titleStmt.title: A transcription of Romans in 010
The New Testament
Das Neue Testament
The Epistle to the Romans
Romans

respStmt.name: The International Greek New Testament Project

titleStmt.funder: This transcription was made as part of the CATENA project, which has received funding from the European Research Council (ERC) under the European Union Horizon 2020 research and innovation programme (grant agreement no. 770816).

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

editionStmt.edition: Version 1.1, created on 03.02.2021, archived on the University of Birmingham eData repository on 13.5.2024.

publisher.name: The International Greek New Testament Project (IGNTP)

availability.p: Attribution 4.0 International (CC BY 4.0) Available for re-use under a creative commons license provided attribution is made to the original creators.

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the International Greek New Testament Project in its work towards the Editio Critica Maior.

editorialDecl.p: This transcription was produced using the online transcription editor developed by the Center for Digital Humanities at the University of Trier as part of the Workspace for Collaborative Editing Project between 2010 and 2013 and made available for use by the Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing and the University of Birmingham.
The XML specifications used are version 1.6 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

body.note: This transcription attempts to keep to the usual word breaks for sense, rather than reproducing the unusual word breaks in the manuscript with interpunction.

ab.note: The word αβρααμ is written with a Latin R throughout this folio.
The letter φ has been rewritten offset to the right.
The smaller delta to guide the rubricator in the left margin has not been deleted.
An enlarged α to make αρα was probably intended but not completed.
An enlarged ν to make νομος was probably intended but not completed.
An enlarged τ to make τι was probably intended but not completed.
The small α in the left margin beginning this word is probably the guide letter for the rubricator and the enlarged letter was forgotten.
The word η is probably a guide letter for the rubricator as it sits in the margin.
This line and the line above may be written over an erasure.
The letter ω is probably a guide letter for the rubricator as it sits in the margin.
The guide letter ω can still be seen to the left of the enlarged ω.
The guide letter for the rubricator can still be seen to the left of the enlarged ε.
The first alpha is a guide letter to aid the rubricator. The enlarged letter was not completed.
An enlarged ο to make οιδαμεν was probably intended but not completed.
An enlarged α to make αλλ was probably intended but not completed.
An enlarged α to make αληθιαν was probably intended but not completed.
An enlarged ο to make ουχ was probably intended but not completed.
The first rho in this word may in fact be a Latin p, meaning σπερμα was intended.
The lunate sigma here may be a Latin c, suggesting ιακοβ may have been intended.
This line and the line above it may be written over an erasure or just a on rough patch of parchment.
The second rho in this word may be a Latin p, suggesting παραπτωματι was intended.
The dots over the words επει, μεν and πεσοντας correspond to dots over words in the Latin column in a different order.
An enlarged π to make παρακαλω was probably intended, but not completed.
Latin text is written above this word.
An enlarged μ for μη was intended here but not completed. The guide letter can still be seen in the left margin.
Latin words above the Greek text here.
The enlarged π to make πασαις was probably intended but not completed. The π in the left margin is likely a guide letter, but could be a later correction.
The enlarged letter μ was missed, but the guide letter can still be seen in the left margin.
The guide letter τ can still be seen in the left margin.
The guide letter σ for adding the enlarged initials can still be seen in the left margin.
The guide letter here may have been ε, but ο was written.
The guide letter ο can be seen in the left margin.
There is Latin text above this word.
There is Latin text above this word.
An enlarged π to make πεπισμαι was not completed, but the guide letter can still be seen in the left margin.
The enlarged ν was intended but not completed. The guide letter can still be seen in the left margin.
The enlarged π was intended but not completed. The guide letter can still be seen in the margin.
The enlarged initial σ was intended but not completed. The guide letter can still be seen in the left margin.
The enlarged initial α was intended but not completed. The guide letter can still be seen in the left margin.
The enlarged π to make παρακαλω was intended but not completed. The guide letter can still be seen in the margin.
